original: Crusaders massacre Jews of Worms
The Principality of Antioch, a crusader state, falls to the Mamluk Sultan Baibars in the Battle of Antioch
After 100 years of Crusader control, the last Crusader stronghold of Acre is reconquered and destroyed by the Mamluks under Sultan al-Ashraf Khalil
Bruges Matins; the nocturnal massacre of the French garrison in Bruges by local Flemish militia
Turkish Armada arrives to begin the Great Siege of Malta, aiming to establish the island as their gateway for the conquest of Europe
Dutch explorer Willem Barents sets off from Amsterdam on his third expedition to try to find a Northwest passage through the Arctic
English colony Massachusetts Bay grants Puritan voting right
Rhode Island enacts the first law declaring slavery illegal
Dutch and English troops occupy Cologne
Great Britain declares war on France at the start of the Seven Years' War
Fire destroys a large part of Montreal, Quebec
First United Empire Loyalists reach Parrtown, Saint John, New Brunswick, Canada after leaving the United States
Battle of Tourcoing; French Republican army defeats a Habsburg coalition bear Lille, France, during War of the First Coalition
"Peace of Amiens" between French Republic and Great Britain ends as Britain declares war on France, due to France's imperialist policies in the West Indies, Italy, and Switzerland
Battle of Las Piedras: The first great military triumph of the revolution of the Río de la Plata in Uruguay lead by Jose Artigas
Edwin Budding of England signs an agreement for the manufacture of his invention, a lawn mower. Saturdays are destroyed forever
United Free Church of Scotland forms
US troops attack Rio Grande occupying Matamoros, during the Mexican–American War
Opening of the first German National Assembly (Nationalversammlung) in Frankfurt, Germany
Amsterdam-Nieuwediep telegraph connection linked
Massachusetts rules all school-age children must attend school
Battle of Sewall's Point, Virginia: First Union offensive action against Confederate forces
Friedrich Hebbel's play "Kriemhildes Rache" premieres in Weimar
Battle of Yellow Bayou, Louisiana (Bayou de Glaize, Old Oaks)
Netherlands government of Isaäc Dignus Fransen De Putte resigns
Robert Tanner Freeman is 1st African American to graduate from Harvard Dental School
Surrender and dissolution of the Ezo Republic to Japan
Emmanuel Chabrier's opera "Le roi malgré lui" (King, in spite of himself) premieres at the Opéra-Comique in Paris; after 3 performances the theatre burns down
US Supreme court affirms legitimacy of racial separation (Plessy v Ferguson), a doctrine that came to be known as "separate but equal"
Irish Music Festival first held in Dublin
New York Giants third baseman Bill Joyce becomes last player in baseball history to hit 4 triples in a game as Giants beat Pittsburgh Pirates, 11-5 at Exposition Park
World Goodwill Day: 26 nations meet at the First Hague Peace Conference
Britain proclaims a protectorate over the Kingdom of Tonga
In Morocco, a brigand, Raizuli, kidnaps Ion H. Perdicaris, an American citizen
Maurits Binger establishes two Dutch movie companies
A British Royal Inquiry into the Easter Rising in Dublin is set up in London
US pilot Kiffin Rockwell shoots down German aircraft over Alsace in WWI
Erik Satie, Leonide Massine and Pablo Picasso's ballet "Parade" premieres in Paris
US Congress passes Selective Service Act, authorizing the federal government to raise a national army for the American entry into World War I through compulsory enlistment
Dutch Indian Volksraad installed in Batavia
TNT explosion in chemical factory in Oakdale, Pennsylvania, kills 200 people
Dutch 2nd Chamber agrees to 48 hour work week (was 45 hrs)
Evangelist Aimee Semple McPherson vanishes in Venice, California She shows up a month later saying she's been kidnapped
Bath School Disaster: Andrew Kehoe blows up Bath Consolidated School killing 38 children, 2 teachers at Bath, Michigan
Grauman's Chinese Theatre opens in Hollywood, California
One of the largest-known fast-moving landslides forms Lower Slide Lake in Gros Ventre, Wyoming
Ritz Hotel opens in Boston, Massachusetts
MLB record 50 runs for a doubleheader: Brooklyn Robins beat Philadelphia Phillies, 20-16 in first game, Phillies win 8-6 in nightcap, at the Baker Bowl in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Around 5,000 forced deportees in the Soviet Union arrive on Nazino Island; within thirteen weeks most of them will be dead due to disease, cannibalism and violence
Trans-Word Airlines (TWA) begins commercial service
WWII: German troops conquer Antwerp, Belgium
Italian army in Ethiopia under general Aosta surrenders to Britain
NYC ends night baseball games for rest of WW II
Allied bombers attack Pantelleria, an Italian island 100 km southwest of Sicily
Expulsion of more than 200,000 Tartars from Crimea by Soviet Union begins, they are accused of collaborating with the Germans
Polish 2nd Army corps captures convent of Monte Cassino, Italy
MLB Detroit Tigers & Philadelphia A's both have 7 straight games postponed due to rain
MLB Philadelphia A's catcher Warren Rosar catches his 147th game without an error
Arab Legion captures fort on Mt Scopus during Arab-Israeli war
Saudi Arabia joins invasion of Israel
The First Legislative Yuan of the Republic of China officially convenes in Nanking
Antiquarian Booksellers Association of America incorporates
MLB St. Louis Cardinals baseman Tommy Glaviano makes 3 consecutive errors on grounders
US General Collins predicts use of atom bomb in Korea
American Jacqueline Cochran becomes the first woman to break the sound barrier
28.7 cm rain falls at Lake Maloya New Mexico (state record)
Atkinson & Depeiaza take West Indies cricket team from 6-187 to 6-494 in a day v Australia
Hungarian party leader Matyas Rákosi enforces his own policy
Queen Julianaopens Rembrandt fairs in Amsterdam, Netherlands
11th Cannes Film Festival: "The Cranes Are Flying" directed by Mikhail Kalatozov wins the Palme d'Or
"Castin' My Spell" by The Johnny Otis Show hits #52
"Judy" by David Seville hits #86
"Russian Band Stand" by Spencer & Spencer hits #91
Eillen Fulton begins playing Lisa on As the World Turns (for almost 30 yrs)
14th Cannes Film Festival: "The Long Absence" directed by Henri Colpi and "Viridiana" directed by Luis Bunuel jointly awarded the Palme d'Or
"If You Wanna Be Happy" by Jimmy Soul hits #1
US Supreme Court rules it unconstitutional to deprive naturalized citizens of citizenship if they return to home country for more than 3 years
WTAF TV channel 29 in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (IND) begins broadcasting
Silver hits a record $1.60 an ounce in London
Tennessee Governor Ellington approves repeal of the Butler Act (or "Monkey Law") - prohibiting the teaching of evolution, upheld in 1925 Scopes Trial
Frank Howard ties AL record with HR in his 6th consecutive game his 10 home runs in the most in 6 games
Apollo 10 launches from Kennedy Space Center and later transmits the 1st color pictures of Earth from space
Beatles' last released LP, "Let It Be", released in US
Bulgarian constitution comes into effect
Vampire rapist Wayne Boden's last victim found in Calgary, Alberta
"Me and The Chimp" an American TV sitcom last airs on CBS in the US
American basketball player John Sebastian makes 63 consecutive free throws while blindfolded
American pop rock band Looking Glass release their single "Brandy"
WIBC Bowling Queens won by Dotty Fothergill
India becomes the sixth nation to detonate an atomic bomb
Nigeria announces 55 percent government participation in all oil concessions
US, USSR and other nations sign the Environmental Modification Convention, prohibiting weather warfare with widespread, long-lasting or severe effects
Italy legalizes abortion
Russian dissident Yuri Orlov exiled to compulsory work
Belgium 3rd government of Martens forms
Fernando Belaunde Terry elected president of Peru
Gwangju Massacre: Students in Gwangju, South Korea begin demonstrations, calling for democratic reforms
People's Republic of China launches 1st intercontinental rocket
Tigers outfielder Larry Herndon is 14th to hit 4 consecutive HRs
Senate revises immigration laws, gives millions of illegal aliens legal status under an amnesty program
First remote location for ABC News "Nightline," South Africa
Chung Kwung Ying does 2,750 "atomic" hand-stand push-ups
David Goch finishes swimming 55,682 miles in a 25-yd pool
South African army occupies Botswana, Zimbabwe & Zambia
In just Oakland's 39th of the season, pitcher Dave Stewart breaks record with his 12th balk en route to 16
"Laugh-In" comedienne arrested at JFK airport in New York on an 11-year-old drug warrant
East Germany and West Germany sign a monetary union treaty, effective July 1
TV movie "Return To Green Acres" airs
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Republic of Somaliland declares independence from Somalia
USSR launches two cosmonauts to Mir space station
45th Cannes Film Festival: "Den goda viljan" directed by Bille August wins the Palme d'Or
US Supreme Court rules states could not force mentally unstable criminal defendants to take anti-psychotic drugs
Italian police arrest Mafia boss Benedetto "Nitto" Santapaola
Israel withdraws from the Gaza Strip
Tropical Butterfly Garden at Cleveland Metroparks Zoo in Ohio opens
WIBC Bowling Queens won by Sandra Postma
WIBC Bowling Queens won by Lisa Wagner
"King David" opens at New Amsterdam Theater NYC
50th Cannes Film Festival: "Taste of Cherry" directed by Abbas Kiarostami and "Unagi" (The Eel) directed by Shohei Imamura jointly awarded the Palme d'Or
United States v. Microsoft: Department of Justice and 20 states file an antitrust case against Microsoft
"Millennium" 3rd studio album by the Backstreet Boys is released - one of the best-selling albums of all time with over 30 million copies sold
101-year-old Harold Stilson becomes oldest golfer to record a hole-in-one when he aces the 108-yard, par-3, 16th hole at Deerfield CC, FL
Saudi Arabia selects the eight foreign companies to take part in its "Gas Initiative," three core venture gas projects that have an anticipated worth of $25 billion
Micky Ward beats Arturo Gatti by majority decision in a junior welterweight boxing bout in Uncasville, Connecticut; 'The Ring' Fight of the Year; first fight in their legendary trilogy
Arizona Diamondbacks' Randy Johnson becomes 16th pitcher to throw a perfect game (2-0 vs Atlanta)
The post Loktantra Andolan government passes a landmark bill curtailing the power of the monarchy and making Nepal a secular country
The Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam are defeated by the Sri Lankan government, ending the Sri Lankan Civil War and almost 26 years of fighting between the two sides
Police officer James Crooker is asked to leave the Red and Black Café in Portland, Oregon after co-owner John Langley claimed Crooker's uniformed presence made him uncomfortable
RC Toulonnais defeat ASM Clermont Auvergne to win the rugby Heineken Cup final
Swiss voters reject a $25 per hour minimum wage
The Tradition Senior Men's Golf, Shoal Creek G&CC: Kenny Perry wins his third of 4 Champions Tour majors by 1 stroke from Mark Calcavecchia
Mexican Damojh airlines flight crashes near Havana airport, Cuba, killing 110 with just 3 survivors
Santa Fe High School shooting; 17 year-old shooter kills 10 and injures 10 in Santa Fe, Texas
WHO member states agree to set up an inquiry into the global response to the pandemic, including looking at the WHO itself
India records the highest recorded daily COVID-19 death toll to date in the world with 4,529 deaths
MLB Detroit Tigers pitcher Spencer Turnbull no-hits Seattle Mariners, 5-0 at Comerica Park, Detroit
Harrison Fordis presented with an honorary Palme d'Or lifetime achievement award at the Cannes Film Festival in France
706 people called Kyle gather in Kyle, Texas, attempting to break the record for a gathering of people with the same name (record is 2,325 Ivans 2017)
Bayer Leverkusen become the first Bundesliga team in history to finish a German league season unbeaten after a 2-1 win at home against Augsburg
Centrist mayor of Bucharest and former mathematics professor Nicusor Dan wins the presidential election in Romania over hard-right nationalist and pro-Russian candidate George Simion
English rock band "The Who" announce the resignation of Zak Starkey, their drummer since 1996; Starkey claims that he did not quit, but was fired - later revising that he instead "had been retired"
Russia launches its largest attack on Ukraine since the beginning of the war with 273 drones
Roman EmperorConstantine the Greatinstitutes free daily bread rations in Constantinople
PlaywrightThomas Kyd's accusations of heresy lead to an arrest warrant for Christopher Marlowe
Dutch jurist and scholarHugo Grotiussentenced to life in prison in Loevestein Castle in the Netherlands (later escapes in a book chest)
John Winthropis elected 1st Governor of the Massachusetts Bay Colony
Ville-Marie (later Montreal), Canada, founded by the Société Notre-Dame de Montréal led by Paul de Chomedey
Napoléon Bonaparte is proclaimed Emperor of France by the French Senate
Famous dandyBeau Brummellflees to France to escape a gambling debt. He never returns, dying in France in 1840.
US Republican Party nominatesAbraham Lincolnfor president
US GeneralUlysses S. Grantbegins siege on Vicksburg, Mississippi; after 47 days of battle siege, Confederate Lt. General John C. Pemberton’s troops surrender

Warren Wagon Train raid: Native American Comanche and Kiowa warriors attack a corn train led by Henry Warren near Graham, Texas, killing 7 waggoners.William T. Shermanpursues and captures 3 leaders, Satanta (White Bear), Satank (Sitting Bear) and Addo-eta ( Big Tree).
Jules Massenet's opera "Esclarmonde" premieres in Paris
Khodynka Tragedy: A stampeding crowd on Khodynka Field, Moscow, during the coronation festivities for Russian TsarNicholas II, causes the deaths of an estimated 1,300 people
Herbert Henry Dowfounds Dow Chemical in Midland, Michigan
Paul Dukas' symphonic scherzo "The Sorcerer’s Apprentice" (used in Disney's Fantasia film) premieres, based on poem byJohann Wolfgang von Goethe
Philadelphia Athletics rout Detroit Tigers, 24-2 at Shibe Park; Tigers use amateur players in protest ofTy Cobb's suspension from the game
First units of the American Expeditionary Force, commanded by GeneralJohn J. Pershing, is ordered to France
Sid Grauman's Chinese Theatre opens in Hollywood, California,  with a stage production followed by the world premiere ofCecil B. DeMille's silent film epic "King of Kings"
Tennessee Valley Act (TVA) Act signed byFDR, to build dams
Queen Julianaopens E55 fair in Amsterdam, Netherlands
Mickey Mantlehits HR from both sides of plate for record 3rd time
Jean Genet's play "Le Balcon" (The Balcony) premieres in Paris
David FrostinterviewsPaul McCartneyon BBC-TV
American engineerRay Dolbyfounds Dolby Laboratories in London, England
Gene Roddenberrysuggests 16 names for Star Trek Captain; they include Kirk
AL Kalinehits his 307th HR, surpassingHank Greenbergas a Tiger
Stanley Cup Final, Chicago Stadium, Chicago, IL:Henri Richardscores twice as Montreal Canadiens beat Chicago Black Hawks for a 4-3 series victory
US PresidentRichard Nixonrejects 60 demands of Congressional Black Caucus
Soviet party leaderLeonid Brezhnevvisits West Germany
Novelty song "The Streak" byRay Stevenshits #1
Menachem Beginbecomes Prime Minister of Israel
Unification Church founder RevSun Myung Moonconvicted of tax evasion
Murray Head's "One Night In Bangkok" single (written byTim Rice, andAbba'sBenny AnderssonandBjörn Ulvaeus) hits #3 in US
Chicago CubsRyne Sandbergends 2nd baseman record 123 errorless game streak
Edmonton right wingJari Kurrisets a record for scoring in a Stanley Cup Finals game with 3 goals and 2 assists in a 7-2 win at Boston Garden
DreamWorks Pictures "Shrek", starringMike Myers,Eddie Murphy, andCameron Diazin voice-over roles, debuts
"Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull" directed bySteven Spielberg, starringHarrison Ford, and produced byGeorge Lucas' Lucasfilms, premiers at the Cannes Film Festival
All of Chile's 34 Roman Catholic bishops offer their resignation toPope Francisin wake of child sex scandal
K-pop boy bandBTSrelease their album "Love Yourself: Tear", sells 135,000 first week
Australian federal elections won byScott Morrison's coalition government in a surprise result
US PresidentDonald Trumpconfirms he is taking controversial drug hydroxychloroquine against COVID-19
Amid a nationwide US baby formula shortage, PresidentJoe Bideninvokes the Defense Production Act, requiring suppliers to fulfil orders to baby formula manufacturers first

Final Indiana Jones film "Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny" premieres at the Cannes Film Festival, starringHarrison FordandPhoebe Waller-Bridge, directed by James Mangold and executive produced bySteven SpielbergandGeorge Lucas
Papal inauguration mass ofPope Leo XIVis held at Saint Peter's Square, Vatican City
John Bellingham, a Liverpool merchant who assassinated British Prime Minister Spencer Perceval on May 11, is hung for the crime at the Old Bailey in London
ProfessorWillard Libbysays England's prehistoric monument of Stonehenge dates back to 1848 BC
Eruption of Mount St. Helens in Washington state triggers the largest landslide in history, killing 57 people and causing over $1 billion in damage
Danish people vote in favor of ratifying the Maastricht Treaty
